segments.text: My heart, O God, is steadfast; I will sing and make music with all my soul.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Awake, harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will praise you, Lord, among the nations; I will sing of you among the peoples.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For great is your love, higher than the heavens; your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let your glory be over all the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Save us and help us with your right hand, that those you love may be delivered.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God has spoken from his sanctuary:
In triumph I will parcel out Shechem and measure off the Valley of Sukkoth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet, Judah is my scepter.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Moab is my washbasin, on Edom I toss my sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Is it not you, God, you who have rejected us and no longer go out with our armies?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Give us aid against the enemy, for human help is worthless.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: With God we will gain the victory, and he will trample down our enemies.
segments.speaker: David
